raw_content:
apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: inventory-service
  description: |
    Inventory and stock management service. Tracks product stock levels, warehouse
    locations, and item classifications across all fulfilment centres.

    ## Responsibilities
    - Exposes a REST API for stock reservation and release (called by order-service)
    - Maintains a product catalogue with per-item handling metadata
    - Tracks item_class per SKU: "standard", "perishable", "hazardous"
    - Publishes low-stock events to Kafka (inventory-low-stock)

    ## Item classification model
    Each product has an item_class field in the products table. Fragile items are not
    yet a first-class concept — they are currently stored as a freeform tag in the
    product metadata JSONB column. To properly support fragile items:
      1. Add "fragile" as a valid item_class enum value (migration on inventory-db)
      2. Expose a new filter on GET /products?item_class=fragile so order-service
         can query fragile stock separately
      3. Add a fragile_stock_level field to the StockReservationResponse so callers
         know whether a reservation covers fragile or standard handling
      4. Update the low-stock Kafka event schema to include item_class

    ## Warehouse handling rules
    Fragile items are stored in zone F of each warehouse. Zone F has a capacity limit
    of 2,000 units per fulfilment centre. Reserving fragile stock must check zone capacity,
    not just total stock count. This logic lives in WarehouseAllocator.allocate().

    Architecture Diagram:
    ```mermaid
    graph TD
      A[Order Service] -->|GET /stock/:id| B(Inventory Service)
      A -->|POST /reserve| B
      B -->|Read/Write| C[(inventory-db Postgres)]
      B -->|Produces| D{Kafka: inventory-low-stock}
    ```
  tags:
    - python
    - fastapi
    - warehouse
    - inventory-domain
spec:
  type: service
  lifecycle: production
  owner: team-logistics
  dependsOn:
    - resource:inventory-db
    - resource:kafka-inventory-topic
  providesApis:
    - api:inventory-rest-api

apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: notification-service
  description: |
    Multi-channel notification service. Consumes order and inventory events from Kafka
    and dispatches SMS, email, and push notifications to customers and internal ops teams.

    ## Responsibilities
    - Consumes orders-created, orders-fulfilled, orders-cancelled from Kafka
    - Sends order confirmation emails (via SendGrid) and SMS (via Twilio)
    - Consumes inventory-low-stock and pages the on-call logistics team
    - Applies per-customer notification preferences (opt-out, channel preference)

    ## Notification templates
    Templates are stored in the notifications-db as Jinja2 strings. Each template is
    keyed by (event_type, channel). Adding a new order type (e.g. fragile) requires:
      1. New templates: fragile_order_confirmation_email, fragile_order_confirmation_sms
         — these must include "handle with care" instructions and fragile item warnings
      2. A new event_type value in the template key enum (notification-db migration)
      3. The orders-created Kafka event must carry a handling_instructions field that
         this service reads to select the right template
      4. Coordination with order-service to ensure the field is populated for fragile orders

    ## SLA
    Notifications must be dispatched within 30 seconds of event consumption.
    Fragile order notifications are considered higher priority — they should be sent
    before the standard fulfilment SLA window begins.

    Architecture Diagram:
    ```mermaid
    graph TD
      A{Kafka: orders-created} -->|Consumes| B(Notification Service)
      C{Kafka: inventory-low-stock} -->|Consumes| B
      B -->|Email| D[SendGrid]
      B -->|SMS| E[Twilio]
      B -->|Read/Write| F[(notifications-db)]
    ```
  tags:
    - nodejs
    - kafka-consumer
    - notifications
    - sendgrid
    - twilio
spec:
  type: service
  lifecycle: production
  owner: team-commerce
  dependsOn:
    - resource:kafka-orders-topic
    - resource:kafka-inventory-topic
    - resource:notifications-db
  providesApis: []

apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: order-service
  description: |
    Core order management service. Handles the full lifecycle of a customer order:
    creation, validation, status transitions, and fulfilment dispatch.

    ## Responsibilities
    - Accepts new orders via REST API from the web-frontend and mobile-gateway
    - Validates item availability by calling inventory-service before confirming
    - Charges customers by delegating to payment-processing-service
    - Publishes order lifecycle events to Kafka (orders-created, orders-fulfilled, orders-cancelled)
    - Stores order state in its own Postgres database (order-db)

    ## Item model
    Each order line carries an item_type field. Current valid values: "standard", "perishable".
    Adding new item types (e.g. "fragile") requires:
      1. A schema migration on order-db (orders table, order_lines table)
      2. A new validation rule in OrderValidator — fragile items must have special_handling=true
      3. A contract change on the orders-created Kafka event (add handling_instructions field)
      4. Coordination with inventory-service (fragile stock is tracked separately)
      5. Coordination with notification-service (fragile orders trigger an additional "handle with care" SMS)

    ## Known fragility concerns
    The OrderValidator is tightly coupled to the item_type enum. Any new item type must be
    added to the ItemType enum in domain/models.py and the corresponding database migration
    must be run before deployment. Rollback requires re-migration.

    Architecture Diagram:
    ```mermaid
    graph TD
      A[Web Frontend] -->|POST /orders| B(Order Service)
      C[Mobile Gateway] -->|POST /orders| B
      B -->|GET /stock/:item_id| D[Inventory Service]
      B -->|POST /charge| E[Payment Processing Service]
      B -->|Produces| F{Kafka: orders-created}
      B -->|Produces| G{Kafka: orders-fulfilled}
      F -->|Consumes| H[Notification Service]
    ```
  tags:
    - java
    - spring-boot
    - kafka-producer
    - critical-path
    - order-domain
spec:
  type: service
  lifecycle: production
  owner: team-commerce
  dependsOn:
    - component:inventory-service
    - component:payment-processing-service
    - resource:kafka-orders-topic
    - resource:order-db
  providesApis:
    - api:order-rest-api

apiVersion: backstage.io/v1alpha1
kind: Component
metadata:
  name: payment-processing-service
  description: |
    Handles all external credit card processing and internal wallet top-ups.

    Architecture Diagram:
    ```mermaid
    graph TD
      A[Web UI] -->|REST API| B(Payment Service)
      B -->|Produces Event| C{Kafka Topic: payments-out}
      B -->|REST API| D[Stripe External API]
    ```
  tags:
    - python
    - fastapi
    - critical-path
spec:
  type: service
  lifecycle: production
  owner: team-finance
  dependsOn:
    - resource:kafka-payments-topic
  providesApis:
    - api:payment-rest-api
